headline: BlackRock OKX Deal Links Tokenized Fund Collateral To Crypto Trading
summary: BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) has entered a new digital asset partnership with crypto exchange OKX. The agreement uses a $2.5b BlackRock institutional money market fund as collateral on OKX, with custody support from Standard Chartered. This structure links a regulated money market product with a major crypto trading venue through bank-grade custody. BlackRock, trading at around $1,056.86 per share, has already seen substantial multi year share price gains, including a 71.1% return over 3 years and...

headline: Franklin Stock Gains as Q2 Earnings Beat Estimates, Revenues Rise Y/Y
summary: BEN's Q2 results reflect an earnings beat and higher revenues, with shares rising 4.1% in early trading despite a slight AUM decline and higher expenses.

headline: BX vs. BLK: Which Asset Manager Wins the Growth Race as Markets Shift?

headline: Invesco Stock Slips as Q1 Earnings Miss Despite Higher AUM & Revenues
